---
title: "Quantitative Methods in Tourism"
subtitle: "Welcome · Course overview"
date: last-modified
---

## About this course

- **6 ECTS · core** module of the *Master in Tourism and Sustainable Development*
- Taught at the **TIDES Institute**, Universidad de Las Palmas de Gran Canaria
- Two blocks, two instructors:
  - **Topics 1 & 2** (Christian González) · *Introduction to R* and *Visual exploratory analysis*
  - **Topics 3 & 4** (Juan Hernández Guerra) · *Machine learning* and *Text classification (NLP)*

::: {.notes}
Quick tour: what students already know in R, why tourism data, what we expect from them.
:::

## What you will learn (Topics 1 & 2)

::: {.incremental}
- Load, inspect, clean and transform tourism data with the tidyverse.
- Produce publishable figures with `ggplot2`.
- Write reproducible reports in Quarto.
- Submit your work via Git and GitHub.
:::

## Schedule · 30 hours, 6 days of 5 h

| Day | Morning | Afternoon |
|:---:|---|---|
| 1 | Git & GitHub · Data in R | Guided exercise |
| 2 | Data wrangling (tidyverse) | Guided exercise |
| 3 | Basic statistics with R | Graded exercise |
| 4 | One-variable visualisation | Graded exercise |
| 5 | Multi-variable visualisation | Graded exercise |
| 6 | Editing reproducible documents | Final submission |

## Course materials

- **Slides** · rendered at <https://chrglez.github.io/quantitative-methods-master-tides/>
- **Source** · <https://github.com/chrglez/quantitative-methods-master-tides>
- **Datasets** · [ISTAC](https://www.gobiernodecanarias.org/istac/) (Canary Islands) and
  [Eurostat tourism](https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/web/tourism/data/database)
- **Licence** · CC BY-SA 4.0

## Grading

- Guided exercises (Days 1–2) · formative, not graded
- Graded exercises (Days 3–5) · 20 % each = **60 %**
- Final submission (Day 6) · **40 %**

All submissions are pushed to each student's fork of the course repository.
Details in the *Git & GitHub essentials* session.

# Let's get started {background-color="#0067a2"}

Welcome aboard.
